title: Green Beans – with butter

seo.title: Green Beans - with butter - Marinda Kook

html:
Green beans are easily a meal on their own for me. It gives colour and joy to any plate of food. I always make too much so there's a spoonful left over for tomorrow's hunger.

  1. 500 g green beans
  2. 60 ml oil
  3. 2 onions
  4. 2 garlic cloves
  5. 250 ml bacon
  6. 2 potatoes, cut into cubes
  7. juice of one lemon
  8. 60 ml Worcester sauce
  9. 5 ml ground nutmeg
  10. salt and pepper to taste
  11. 10 ml Aromat (if you have a conscientious objection to it – leave it out)
  12. 80 ml butter

NOW:

  • slice your onion into rings and fry in your 60 ml oil until glossy in a pot or pan on top of the stove
  • add the bacon and finely chopped garlic and fry further
  • cut the potatoes into cubes like little dice and put them together with the beans in your pan.
  • season with all the rest of the ingredients except the butter
  • now you must taste and add more of what you like
  • once your green beans are soft, but not mushy, add the butter and stir it through

Enjoy the green beans as a side dish or on their own. I'm going to eat my green beans today with rib slow-roasted in the oven.
